seoTitle: How to Add or Remove Your Accountant in QuickBooks
metaDescription: Learn how to add or remove your accountant's access in QuickBooks Online, so you can control who sees your financial data and manage permissions.
intro: Giving your accountant the right level of access to QuickBooks Online — and knowing how to remove it later — is part of managing your business's financial security. This guide covers both adding and removing an accountant in QuickBooks Online, walking through the navigation involved in each. It's meant for small business owners and accountants who need to control who can see and edit financial data.

steps.heading: Navigate to accountant settings
steps.body: Open the section of QuickBooks Online where you manage who has accountant-level access to your account.

steps.heading: Add an accountant
steps.body: Grant a new accountant access by adding their details, so they can view and work within your books.

steps.heading: Remove an accountant when needed
steps.body: If an accountant no longer needs access — for example, after ending an engagement — remove them from that same settings area.

steps.heading: Follow user access best practices
steps.body: Review who has access periodically, granting it only to people who currently need it to keep your financial data secure.

steps.heading: Combine with automation tools
steps.body: Once access is set correctly, you can use QuickBooks Online's automation features to let your accountant handle routine bookkeeping more efficiently.

faq.question: Why would I need to remove an accountant from QuickBooks Online?
faq.answer: You might remove an accountant after ending a working relationship or switching providers, to keep access limited to people currently working on your books.

faq.question: Is adding an accountant different from adding a regular user?
faq.answer: Yes, accountant access is a distinct role in QuickBooks Online designed specifically for accounting professionals working on your account.

faq.question: Can I add more than one accountant?
faq.answer: QuickBooks Online supports managing accountant access for the professionals you work with, though specifics depend on your plan.

faq.question: Does removing an accountant delete any data?
faq.answer: Removing an accountant revokes their access; it does not delete your financial records or historical data.

faq.question: Who typically needs to manage accountant access?
faq.answer: Small business owners and larger organizations that work with external or in-house accountants need this feature to control data access securely.
